name: Highland Oatcakes
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift salt and oatmeal into a large bowl.
2. Place the gridle or heavy frying pan over medium heat.
3. Heat the fat in the pan until it begins to boil.
4. Add hot water to create a well in the center of the oatmeal mixture.
5. Mix gently until a stiff dough forms, then divide into two portions.
6. Roll each portion out on a floured surface to the size of a dinner plate and about 1/8-inch thick.
7. Cut into quarters or farles (small pieces).
8. Check the gridle's heat by placing your hand briefly above it.
9. Place one quartered round onto the heated gridle.
10. When the oatcakes are ready, the surface will stop steaming and become dry and white.
11. Flip and cook the other side until lightly browned in a hot oven or under the grill—ensure they curl up towards the heat source.
Source: Elisabeth Luard in "Country Living" (British), February 1989.
